text:
Market today: Chips are the story - the headline about a $2 trillion Q2 rally in Micron, Intel, and AMD confirms the sector tailwind that's been lifting your book. Sentiment is risk-on in tech/semis, which is exactly your neighborhood.

Your holdings: Everything is green and no scanner SELL/TRIM flags - all look fine today. One note: SOXL (up 4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF with real decay risk - gains can reverse hard and fast on margin. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF (up 1.91%) - treat it as tiny-size only; margin amplifies any gap-down badly.

Watch: (1) Whether NVDA and AMAT hold their intraday levels - they're the gravitational center for SMH and SOXL. (2) Any macro surprise from the Trump economic team reshuffling (Pierre Yared exit) that could spook rates/growth sentiment.

Bottom line: Your book is in great shape on a great tape - your only job today is protecting gains, especially on the leveraged names.
plays: SOXL: It's up 4.8% and leveraged 3x - consider tightening your stop toward 220-230 to lock in more of the gain; default stop at 200 is wide on a margin account.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, very volatile - keep size tiny and respect the 21.38 stop hard; margin plus leverage plus a new issue is a dangerous combo if tape turns.

No new buys today - scanner shows ALAB (9/12, entry 481.96-482.54, stop 479.73) as the cleanest setup, but price is flagged as chasing in daily premium; wait for a pullback toward its order block before considering.
